hard.next_step: Ferrero is in active market-exploration to replace SAP Ariba in a phased manner, driven by Ariba's lack of AI/agentic capability. Planned July 2026 discovery session to lock pain, metrics, scope, decision criteria/process and budget owner, then a tailored Merlin Intake + ANA + S2P demo with a phased coexistence-to-replacement roadmap, and multi-thread into IT for SAP ERP integration scoping.
hard.ais_why: Attended the Luxembourg AI World Tour and confirmed they want to replace Ariba in a phased manner due to its lack of AI capabilities; specifically interested in ANA and Intake.

north_star_verdict.headline: Freshly qualified Ferrero Ariba-replacement with strong AI appetite and 9 senior leaders engaged; the gate to advance is a recorded discovery that locks pain, the economic buyer, and a champion.
north_star_verdict.math: Created and qualified 29 Jun 2026 (7 days old); 268 days to a 31 Mar 2027 close leaves ample runway for an early Qualified deal. Recent event-level engagement (02 Jun 2026 Luxembourg Agentic AI roundtable and 29 Jun 2026 kickoff with 9 senior leaders) shows live interest; the SF LastActivityDate field (26 Aug 2025) is stale and predates creation, a data quirk, not a 309-day silence. No submission dates are set, so time-in-stage starts now. Pipeline forecast is appropriate and defensible at this stage.
evidence: Opportunity created and moved Initial Interest -> Qualified on 29 Jun 2026, reassigned to Caroline Lacocque (SF field history)
Two recent event-level touchpoints: 02 Jun 2026 Agentic AI in Procurement Roundtable, Luxembourg and 29 Jun 2026 kickoff with 9 senior procurement leaders (Next_Step__c)
AIS Strong (80): attended the Luxembourg AI World Tour and confirmed intent to replace SAP Ariba in a phased manner for its lack of AI, with specific interest in ANA and Intake (AIS_Why)
Both the kickoff and roundtable were event-level and NOT recorded, so no named buyer has quantified the Ariba pain, decision criteria, or process (Next_Step__c)
No champion advocating and no economic buyer / budget owner mapped across the 5 OpportunityContactRole contacts (OpportunityContactRole)

metrics.narrative: No quantified impact has been captured. AIS_Why and Next_Step__c state Ferrero wants to replace SAP Ariba in a phased manner due to its lack of AI capabilities, a qualitative motivation with no cost, cycle-time, headcount, error-rate, or savings figure attached. The 29 Jun 2026 kickoff with 9 procurement leaders and the 02 Jun 2026 Luxembourg Agentic AI roundtable were event-level and unrecorded, so no buyer quantified the pain on a call. Quantifying tail-spend negotiation, sourcing-cycle, and contract-handling impact is the explicit job of the planned July 2026 discovery session.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in any OpportunityContactRole or synthesis field. Jenna Van Thiel (Global Head of Procurement Excellence) is the primary Decision Maker and Mattia Sturlese (Head of Global Procurement Competence Center) is also flagged Decision Maker, but neither is confirmed to own the S2P budget. The 29 Jun 2026 kickoff drew 9 senior leaders yet was unrecorded, so budget authority was never observed. Identifying and reaching the budget owner, likely at or above Jenna Van Thiel, is a ranked next move.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's stated lead criterion is AI/agentic capability: Ferrero attended the Luxembourg AI World Tour and is specifically interested in ANA and Intake to modernize procurement beyond SAP Ariba (AIS_Why, Strong/80). Phased coexistence-to-replacement and SAP ERP integration are implied criteria from Next_Step__c but none is buyer-quoted on a recorded call. The formal weighted criteria, any BRD or RFP, and the shortlist remain uncaptured pending the July 2026 discovery session.

decision_process.narrative: The process is at its earliest stage: the opportunity was created and moved Initial Interest -> Qualified on 29 Jun 2026 and reassigned to Caroline Lacocque. Engagement so far is two event-level touchpoints (02 Jun 2026 roundtable, 29 Jun 2026 kickoff of 9 leaders) with no recorded working session, so the evaluation stages, timeline, and approval chain are undocumented. Locking the decision process and timeline is an explicit goal of the planned discovery session.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible: no MSA/SOW, legal, procurement channel, or InfoSec signal, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null so there is no forcing function. The 13-module S2P scope plus SAP ERP integration implies a formal IT and InfoSec gate will emerge later, and Next_Step__c already calls for multi-threading into IT for SAP ERP integration scoping. Paper visibility is expected to be dark at Qualified stage 268 days from a 31 Mar 2027 close.

identify_pain.narrative: The articulated pain is that SAP Ariba lacks AI/agentic capability, blocking Ferrero's push to modernize guided buying, tail-spend negotiation, sourcing speed, contract handling, and supplier-risk visibility (AIS_Why, Strong/80; Next_Step__c). This is a confirmed qualitative driver but not yet quantified and not attributed to a named buyer on a recorded call. The likely pain owners are Mattia Sturlese (Head of Global Procurement Competence Center) and Roberta Bassani (Head of Procurement Digital Hub), both present at the 02 Jun 2026 roundtable and 29 Jun 2026 kickoff.

champion.narrative: No champion is identified and no advocacy is evidenced. Roberta Bassani (Head of Procurement Digital Hub, Influencer, and the only task/activity contact) is the strongest candidate, having appeared at the 02 Jun 2026 roundtable and 29 Jun 2026 kickoff, but neither meeting was recorded so her advocacy and access to power are unproven. Mattia Sturlese and Jenna Van Thiel are Decision Makers, not confirmed advocates. Securing a champion ranks ahead of any commercial step.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent Ferrero is exploring to replace in a phased manner, driven by Ariba's lack of AI/agentic capability (Competitors__c: SAP Ariba; Next_Step__c, 29 Jun 2026). No other vendor (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua) has surfaced in any field or activity, so this reads as an early incumbent-displacement rather than a confirmed multi-vendor bake-off. The real threat is Ariba's SAP ERP integration lock-in and status-quo inertia, not a rival's active preference; our wedge is Merlin Intake + ANA agentic AI on a phased coexistence-to-replacement roadmap. The full competitive set must be locked in the July 2026 discovery session.

critical_signals.text: Ferrero is exploring to displace incumbent SAP Ariba because it lacks AI, with no rival vendor yet named, so today's fight is Ariba's SAP lock-in and status-quo inertia, not a bake-off (29 Jun 2026).
The 29 Jun 2026 kickoff drew 9 senior procurement leaders including the Global Head of Procurement Excellence, but was event-level and unrecorded, leaving no quantified pain baseline.
Jenna Van Thiel, Global Head of Procurement Excellence, is the primary Decision Maker on record, engaged at the kickoff but not yet worked directly.
No budget owner is confirmed and no pricing or ROI has been discussed on a 250k, 13-module S2P scope, so the commercial case is entirely unproven.

deal_movement.summary: Brand-new opportunity: created and qualified 29 Jun 2026 (7 days old) and reassigned to Caroline Lacocque. Recent event-level engagement (02 Jun 2026 roundtable, 29 Jun 2026 kickoff with 9 leaders) shows live interest, but neither was recorded so no discovery baseline exists. The SF LastActivityDate field (26 Aug 2025, an old intro call) is stale and predates creation, not a real silence.

items.change: Kickoff held with 9 senior Ferrero procurement leaders (event-level, not recorded)

competitive_position.summary: Single named competitor: SAP Ariba, the incumbent Ferrero is exploring to replace in a phased manner because it lacks AI/agentic capability (Competitors__c; Next_Step__c, 29 Jun 2026). No other vendor has surfaced, so the current threat is not a rival's preference but Ariba's SAP ERP integration lock-in and status-quo inertia (a phased-replacement posture can stall into do-nothing). Our wedge is Merlin Intake + ANA agentic AI on a coexistence-to-replacement roadmap. The full competitive set is unconfirmed and must be locked in the July 2026 discovery session.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified deal with strong AI appetite but no recorded discovery, no champion, and no mapped power. The highest-impact levers now are converting event interest into a recorded discovery, securing an advocate, and finding the budget owner.
flags: Run a RECORDED discovery to quantify Ariba pain and lock decision criteria/process: the 29 Jun kickoff and 02 Jun roundtable were both event-level and unrecorded, so no buyer-quoted baseline exists.
Convert Roberta Bassani (Head of Procurement Digital Hub) or a Decision Maker (Jenna Van Thiel / Mattia Sturlese) into an active champion: 9 leaders engaged but none is advocating for Zycus.
Map the economic buyer / budget owner early: broad seniority is engaged but no one holds confirmed S2P budget authority.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified or developed. Roberta Bassani (Head of Procurement Digital Hub, Influencer and sole task/activity contact) is the strongest candidate, present at the 02 Jun 2026 roundtable and 29 Jun 2026 kickoff, but neither meeting was recorded so her advocacy and access to the economic buyer are unproven. Mattia Sturlese and Jenna Van Thiel are Decision Makers, not confirmed advocates. Champion-building is the critical early gap for this $250k deal.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Very strong AI appetite that is not yet activated: AIS Strong (80), attendance at the Luxembourg AI World Tour, and explicit interest in ANA and Intake, but no tailored Merlin demo has been delivered and the agentic story has not landed with a named buyer on a recorded call. The planned Merlin Intake + ANA + S2P demo is the activation move.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS Strong (80): Ferrero attended the Luxembourg AI World Tour and confirmed they want to replace Ariba in a phased manner due to its lack of AI, with specific interest in ANA and Intake. AI is tied to a real modernization question (guided buying, tail-spend negotiation, sourcing speed, contract handling, supplier risk), not a generic topic. No recorded call yet exists to confirm or over/under-rate the field.

items.detail: No economic buyer / budget owner mapped among the 5 contacts; broad seniority engaged but power and spend authority unconfirmed.
Incumbent SAP Ariba with ERP lock-in plus a phased-replacement posture creates real status-quo/do-nothing risk: Ferrero could keep running Ariba and defer the switch.
SAP ERP integration for the 13-module S2P scope is unscoped; IT has not been multi-threaded yet.

scope_change.detail: No reduction in module scope; the phased approach is an implementation sequence, not a scope cut. Watch for a narrowing to a point-solution (ANA/Intake only) if the July discovery reveals budget constraints.

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won found on Ferrero; treat as net-new (New Markets division). Executive/EB access is NOT inherited, so mapping the budget owner is a genuine early-stage gap here.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Freshly qualified Ferrero S2P (created 29 Jun 2026): a strong AI-led SAP Ariba-replacement story with 9 senior procurement leaders engaged at the 29 Jun kickoff, but no recorded discovery, no champion, and no economic buyer mapped yet.

win_position.text: Early but real: Ferrero is actively exploring to replace SAP Ariba for its AI gaps and is specifically interested in ANA and Intake (AIS Strong/80), with two Decision Makers on record and 9 senior leaders at the 29 Jun 2026 kickoff.
No one is carrying the deal yet: no champion is advocating internally and no economic buyer or budget owner is mapped across the 5 contacts (OpportunityContactRole, 29 Jun 2026).
Every touchpoint (02 Jun roundtable, 29 Jun kickoff) was event-level and unrecorded, so the Ariba pain, criteria, and process are still unvalidated by any named buyer.

deal_momentum.text: Fresh forward motion: created and moved Initial Interest -> Qualified on 29 Jun 2026 with a same-week kickoff of 9 senior procurement leaders (field history; Next_Step__c).
Momentum is rep-planned, not buyer-pulled: the July 2026 discovery and tailored demo are Zycus's next steps, and the customer has not named a date or requested the next meeting.

customer_commitment.text: Strong stated intent: Ferrero attended the Luxembourg AI World Tour and confirmed they want to phase out Ariba due to its lack of AI, focusing on ANA and Intake (AIS_Why, Strong/80).
Commitment is event-level only: no BRD, timeline, or procurement process has been shared, so intent has not yet converted into a structured evaluation.

deal_risk.text: Incumbent SAP Ariba's ERP lock-in plus a phased-replacement posture creates real do-nothing/status-quo risk on a 31 Mar 2027 close (Next_Step__c, 29 Jun 2026).
Blind on power and advocacy: no champion and no confirmed budget owner among the 5 mapped Ferrero contacts (OpportunityContactRole).

champion.evidence: No champion identified; Roberta Bassani a candidate but advocacy unproven (OpportunityContactRole, 29 Jun 2026)

economic_buyer.evidence: No economic buyer or budget owner named in any contact role or field
